title: Axonopus ulei
taxonomy: Axonopus ulei is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of perennial grass native to the Americas. The genus Axonopus is placed in the subfamily Panicoideae, which includes several species of grasses. Axonopus ulei is further classified as a member of the tribe Paniceae, a group of grasses with a worldwide distribution. The classification of Axonopus ulei reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses and its unique characteristics.

It is believed to be effective in treating various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems. Additionally, Axonopus ulei may be used to soothe digestive issues and reduce pain. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to help manage conditions such as diabetes and skin infections.
